threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver talks about 'getting into' the 30 instead of just catching it. Proper bus lingo takes time.
Caught a late 333 last week; a bloke serenading the driver with a guitar. Everyone tried to ignore him, but it was oddly soothing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy in a tutu selling falafel outside the U-Bahn on a rainy Tuesday. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a Späti with an open-air cinema in the back, complete with weird indie films and the scent of döner wafting through.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they navigate around BART delays like a seasoned sailor in fog—straight to the taco truck instead of waiting at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the N-Judah with an accordion at Powell, locals tapping their feet while tourists just looked confused. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but they miss the tiny bars tucked away in the back streets where locals gather for conversations that last all night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confusion over the Mercat de Gràcia’s layout – locals know the secret shortcuts and hidden gems.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say ‘thank you’ to the bus driver. True locals just wave and keep moving on.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with his guitar. Must've been 3 a.m. and somehow it felt like a private gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I caught the scent of curry and grilled sausage mingling in the air at the Turkish market on Hermannstraße. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find abandoned subway stations turned into art spaces. The weird murals down at ‘Pankow’ are a must-see for anyone curious.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the 16th Street BART station on Fridays after 5—it's a complete madhouse with overheated train brakes and gridlock.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the bus while the driver was stuck in traffic. Everyone was more amused than annoyed. Just another day in the city!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks, but it's the back streets buzzing with late-night cervezas that show the real spirit.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they look confusedly at the bus timetable—like it’s a puzzle instead of a schedule. No one takes the time to understand it, right?
